RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT VIC YANG'S Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Interrogatories, Document, Demands And Deposition Questions Beyond The Scope Of Discovery -Defendant Yang's Motion for Protective Order is DENIED, without prejudice to Defendant to refile his motion at a later date. California Rule of Court, Rule 3.1345, requires a separate statement for any motion involving the content of a discovery request or responses to such a request. No separate statement was filed. However, in light of the multiple discovery requests, and at least one prior discovery order pertaining to these requests, separate statements from the parties would assist in determining which specific discovery issues need to be decided. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not Reported.
